surface.name: Dynatrace Playground sample-data exploration
surface.selection_basis: Under the workshop policy, Playground is the fastest documented route: Dynatrace calls it a public sandbox for exploring sample data without installing software, while the trial routes require account provisioning plus OneAgent or OpenTelemetry ingestion. Playground signup is treated as one opaque step at the official URL.

alternatives_considered.name: OpenTelemetry ingestion
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The general guide presents OpenTelemetry beside OneAgent and does not select it for every infrastructure.

alternatives_considered.name: OneAgent trial ingestion
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: It requires a trial environment, administrator-controlled host, token, download, installation, and process restart; Playground reaches a working exploration sooner.

entry_point.developer_intent: Use the public Dynatrace Playground to explore and interact with live dashboards, notebooks, and sample observability data without installing software.
assumed_prior_state: No Dynatrace account, trial environment, tenant access, API token, OneAgent installation, or monitored host is assumed.
For the downstream OneAgent candidate, the developer controls a clean Linux, Windows, or AIX host with administrator access and SSL-capable network connectivity.
No signup, environment creation, token generation, software download, installation, process restart, or data ingestion was performed during research.

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The developer opens the Dynatrace Playground and interacts with a live dashboard or notebook backed by sample data.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: A Playground dashboard or notebook loads and displays interactive sample observability data.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: Explore and interact with sample data in Playground, the public sandbox.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: This is the first documented usable Dynatrace state and requires no software installation or data ingestion. Trial ingestion of the developer's own environment is a later alternative.

prerequisites.requirement: Complete the official Playground signup or use an existing Dynatrace account whose environment switcher exposes Playground.
Use a web browser; no agent installation is required.

primary_path.action: Open Discover Dynatrace and select Playground.
details: The docs describe Playground as a public sandbox with sample data and no software installation.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Use the trial route only if own-environment ingestion is required.

primary_path.action: Sign up for Playground or sign in to an existing Dynatrace account.
details: Treat signup as one opaque workshop-policy step.
primary_path.success_signal: Playground can be opened directly or from the environment switcher.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Follow the recovery instructions on the official account page.

primary_path.action: Open a Playground use case, live dashboard, or notebook.
details: The docs name Kubernetes monitoring, synthetic tests, live dashboards, notebooks, and new-feature exploration as available sample-data experiences.
primary_path.success_signal: A dashboard or notebook loads with sample data and responds to interaction.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Choose another published Playground use case if a specific sample is unavailable.

primary_path.action: Verify that sample data can be explored without installing OneAgent or ingesting OpenTelemetry data.
primary_path.success_signal: Sample data is visible and interactive.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Return to Playground through the environment switcher.

branches.path: Install OneAgent on an administrator-controlled Linux, Windows, or AIX host and verify the host in the Hosts table.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds OS, architecture, monitoring-mode, token, download, signature, installer, restart, and host-verification actions.

branches.path: Follow the linked OpenTelemetry ingestion route and then explore detected services or Smartscape.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes prerequisites, configuration, ingestion protocol, and verification surface.

branches.condition: The developer is not ready to install or ingest
branches.path: Open the public Playground and explore sample data.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Avoids trial and infrastructure setup but demonstrates a sandbox rather than successful ingestion of the developer's data.

friction_gates.description: The public trial endpoint does not expose the required identity, agreement, account, tenant, or environment transition.
The official general guide leaves OneAgent versus OpenTelemetry ingestion to the developer.
OneAgent requires administrator access to a clean supported host.
OneAgent setup generates an API token that cannot be accessed again after the generation screen.
Running processes must be restarted because OneAgent cannot inject itself into them while they are already running.
